CONTACT.ADDRESS: 36 San Pedro St., La Cruz neighborhood, Celaya, GTO 38028, Guanajuato, Mexico

FORM.HEADING: Join the Association / Request Information:
FORM.DESCRIPTION: The main purpose of the association is to promote and highlight the industrial and professional fabric within the construction sector, companies and organizations working to define a new model of industrialized construction (off-site, prefabricated, sustainable and meeting circular economy criteria), fostering competitiveness and collaboration among member associates within the construction sector.

FOOTER.DIRECTION: 36 San Pedro St., La Cruz neighborhood, Celaya, GTO 38028, Guanajuato, Mexico

NEWSLETTER.TITLE: Welcome to the Future of Construction
NEWSLETTER.SUBTITLE: Be part of the change. Connect. Transform. Build.
NEWSLETTER.BODY:
The Industrialized Construction Cluster of Mexico is not just a business network: it is the hub where those who are redesigning the sector converge. Joining ClusterCIIM means entering an active innovation community, where experience meets disruption and real opportunities for collaboration, visibility and growth open up.

Here you connect with leading companies in the sector and a diverse range of visionary SMEs and professionals. We are rethinking every construction process for industrialization, seeking to improve efficiency and sustainability. From new products and modular systems to algorithms that think faster than regulations, the ecosystem moves forward… and you can be part of it.

If you believe that industrialization, data and applied intelligence can transform the way we build, you are in the right place. If you are looking to combine capabilities, share projects or scale your impact, join us today.

Pre-register, I will get in touch with you and send you detailed information to access our network.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Industrialization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Any actor, company, association or professional who wishes to contribute to the process of improvement and modernization of the sector. Not only industrial companies, but also suppliers, SMEs, or professionals who, through their experience, products or solutions, can contribute to the common goal.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Digitalization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: This layer of applications to a sector resistant to digital technology. Sensorizing buildings, facilities or any construction to evolve the concept of home automation and bring it to a level of alignment with available technology.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Deep Digitalization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Within the transformation process driven by ClusterCIIM, there is an emerging vision we call deep digitalization, summarized in the concept MAIA: Modular + Algorithms + Artificial Intelligence. This approach allows rethinking the construction cycle from the ground up, connecting design, manufacturing, operation, commercialization and administration through intelligent data flows and adaptive modular structures. Based on advances in algorithmic design and procedural co-creation platforms, it shows how not only processes are optimized, but the door is opened to a more evolutionary, connected and resilient construction.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Sustainability
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Applying the latest trends and solutions to build passive or energy-positive buildings. We must be an advocate for new green energies and consumption reduction. Construction may be the sector with the greatest potential for improvement in carbon footprint, circular economy and waste reduction.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Training
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: A new way of building requires a new way of teaching. Trades are the evolution of those we already know, but new ones are emerging. Professionals' knowledge must evolve with the market and new realities. That is why Training is a crucial issue in the sector's transformation. We want to work with universities, vocational training centers, schools and companies to define and train the new professionals.

CO_FACTORY.TITLE: A new way of working
CO_FACTORY.DESCRIPTION:
Co-Factory is an industrial space. It provides a manufacturing infrastructure that allows access to projects that exceed the productive and technical capacity of companies.

We are committed to creating a productive space, with teams specifically trained for industrialization, technical infrastructure and strategic support.

BANNER.TITLE: Co-Factory
BANNER.SUBTITLE: We drive the industrialization of construction

SECTION.TITLE:
Asset Investment
Operations Investment
SECTION.DESCRIPTION:
We foster a collaborative space with ClusterCIIM SMEs, startups and industry professionals. Our public-private collaboration is essential for business development.

At ClusterCIIM we do not only work on short-term solutions, but have created a collaboration and debate tool to find the solutions needed by a sector that must transform itself.

BANNER.TITLE: Co-Share
BANNER.SUBTITLE: The cluster organizes bimonthly events with partners in iconic venues, fostering networking.

SECTION.TITLE: Co-Share
SECTION.DESCRIPTION:
In that manifesto, the Federal Government of Mexico and the Government of the State of Querétaro set a challenge:

'Create a BIM Working Group to help implement the culture, values, working methods, digital and electronic technologies to train industry, public administration, research centers, professional associations and the academic world'.

Since then, the Construimos el Futuro Commission has directed its activities in this direction.

BANNER_INFO.TITLE: Industrialization experiences
BANNER_INFO.DESCRIPTION: These events are linked to the CCiC and its associates, offering collaboration opportunities with entities with potential projects, external guests who share experiences aligned with CCiC objectives, and visits to industrial spaces to understand sustainable processes, solutions and digitalization.

CO_SHARE.FOOTER: The gatherings offer a unique platform to learn, collaborate and explore advances in the industry.

SECTION.TITLE: Co-Mentor
SECTION.DESCRIPTION: We accompany companies going through an organizational transition, drawing on the experience of Cluster members who have driven disruptive change.

BANNER_INFO.DESCRIPTION: We offer 5 two-hour meetings with two Cluster mentors to get to know the company: Structure, product, market, channels, financial position. We carry out an internal analysis and a short, medium and long-term strategic plan. It also includes a LEAN Startup agreement to test new models and analyze impacts, and the validation and adaptation of the model, including the possibility of financing.

CO_MENTOR.FOOTER: This accompaniment seeks to be a practical boost for companies in transformation.

TEAM.TITLE: A great team
TEAM.DESCRIPTION:
The Industrialized Construction Cluster of Mexico (ClusterCIIM) was born as a coordinated response from the country's leading public and private stakeholders to drive a stable space for debate, action and real transformation of the construction sector. With the support of key entities such as the Chamber of Commerce of Mexico, the National Business Council and the Government of Mexico, ClusterCIIM has consolidated itself as the meeting point where the master lines of the country's constructive future are defined.

Since its inception, the cluster has directed its activity towards the deep modernization of processes, with special attention to the integration of digital technologies, new organizational models and industrialized systems. This collective commitment to structural transformation — based on innovation, sustainability and collaboration — has made ClusterCIIM the reference space for all those initiatives that want to rethink how the habitat of the future is designed, manufactured and managed.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Héctor Miranda Martínez
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in road construction with extensive experience in planning, execution and quality control in infrastructure projects in the public and industrial sectors.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Osvaldo Mireles
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in technology and sustainable development with specialization in Smart Cities, BIM, Blockchain, AI and the New Urban Agenda.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Jorge Moreno Vargas
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in disruptive technologies, MIT Certified. 13 years leading innovation in smart cities, IoT and sustainable projects.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Teresita de Jesus Guerrero Escamilla
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Civil Engineer, graduated from the Autonomous University of Querétaro in 2004. Her training and career reflect her commitment to engineering and the development of civil projects.

TRANSFORMATION_OF_THE_CONSTRUCTION_SECTO.TITLE: Transformation of the construction sector
TRANSFORMATION_OF_THE_CONSTRUCTION_SECTO.DESCRIPTION:
The Industrialized Construction Cluster of Mexico (ClusterCIIM) was born from the union of professionals with solid and complementary backgrounds: from health applied to housing to architectural innovation, applied engineering, energy efficiency and industrial management; this strategic alliance allows us to shape a space for transformation.

We are committed to a new construction model based on coordination, digitalization and efficiency, using algorithmic methodologies alongside existing BIM and CAD tools, which allows the integration of customized industrialized processes for each company from conceptualization to key handover.

The world is digital and automated; procedural co-creation platforms that explore the intersection between systems and construction details, technology, design, sustainability, marketing and monitoring are tools that bring new perspectives on deep changes in the way we build.

With this convergence of visions, we consolidate ourselves as a key player to lead the paradigm shift in the construction sector in Mexico, with an open, integrative and forward-looking approach prepared to face the challenges of a world in constant evolution.

CONEXION_CREATING_COLLABORATIONS.TITLE: Connection, creating collaborations
CONEXION_CREATING_COLLABORATIONS.DESCRIPTION:
We act as a connector within the industrialized construction ecosystem, creating spaces where companies, professionals, public administrations and knowledge centers can come together to share challenges, visions and strategies. Thanks to the diversity and experience of its members, an active network of trust is created that facilitates the emergence of real collaborations.

These connections become tangible collaborations, shared projects and strategic alliances that allow complex challenges to be addressed from a collective approach. We create a bridge between innovation and action, fostering a collaborative culture in which different perspectives converge with one common purpose: to transform the sector towards a more efficient, sustainable and future-ready construction.
